title: Accumulator Functor
prompt_md:
## Goal

Write a functor class `Accumulator` -- a class with an overloaded `operator()` -- that keeps a running total in a member variable. Each call adds the given value to the stored total and returns the new total.

## Input

- Line 1: an integer `n` (1 <= n <= 100).
- Line 2: `n` space-separated integers (-10000 <= each <= 10000).

## Output

- `n` lines: after reading each value, print the running total so far.

The accumulator must carry its total as state between calls (that is the point of a functor versus a plain function). Construct one `Accumulator` and call it like a function for each input value.

title: Custom Sort with a Functor
prompt_md:
## Goal

Write a functor `AbsCompare` that compares two integers by absolute value, then use an instance of it with `std::stable_sort` to sort a vector by ascending absolute value. Ties (equal absolute values) keep their original relative order -- that is what `std::stable_sort` guarantees.

## Input

- Line 1: an integer `n` (1 <= n <= 100).
- Line 2: `n` space-separated integers (-10000 <= each <= 10000).

## Output

- One line of the `n` integers sorted by ascending absolute value, space-separated, with a trailing newline.

`AbsCompare::operator()(int a, int b) const` must return `true` when `|a| < |b|`. Compute absolute value yourself (do not use `std::abs` or `<cmath>`).
